digest.component_or_area: parent-first claim/transition cascade concurrency policy and race tests
propositions: No test exists for a cross-process/two-clone race where two agents work different children of the same open parent.
The ownership semantics for same-parent/different-child races must be documented before tests can assert them.

In the same-tracker serialized case, agent B seeing the parent already in_progress does not cascade and may claim its child independently under its own assignee; parent ownership does not constrain sibling child assignees.
In the two-offline-clones case, parent and child status forks are resolved independently per ticket by the reducer's UUID tie-break, so a child winner may differ from the parent winner.
A losing parent claim leaves the losing agent's already-written parent/child claim in place (orphaned), not rolled back or tombstoned, signaled via STATUS_FORK_RESOLVED on the affected ticket.
Two new race tests must encode both sub-cases and confirm lock serialization, deterministic convergence, and that lint/typecheck/test pass.
